sEn: Pointer Networks use attention as a pointer to select input elements as output, enabling solutions to problems with variable-size output dictionaries.
sZh: 指针网络利用注意力机制作为指针，从输入序列中选择元素作为输出，从而解决输出字典大小可变的问题。
absEn: We introduce a new neural architecture to learn the conditional probability of an output sequence with elements that are discrete tokens corresponding to positions in an input sequence. Such problems cannot be trivially addressed by existent approaches such as sequence-to-sequence and Neural Turing Machines, because the number of target classes in each step of the output depends on the length of the input, which is variable. Problems such as sorting variable sized sequences, and various combinatorial optimization problems belong to this class. Our model solves the problem of variable size output dictionaries using a recently proposed mechanism of neural attention. It differs from the previous attention attempts in that, instead of using attention to blend hidden units of an encoder to a context vector at each decoder step, it uses attention as a pointer to select a member of the input sequence as the output. We call this architecture a Pointer Net (Ptr-Net). We show Ptr-Nets can be used to learn approximate solutions to three challenging geometric problems -- finding planar convex hulls, computing Delaunay triangulations, and the planar Travelling Salesman Problem -- using training examples alone. Ptr-Nets not only improve over sequence-to-sequence with input attention, but also allow us to generalize to variable size output dictionaries. We show that the learnt models generalize beyond the maximum lengths they were trained on. We hope our results on these tasks will encourage a broader exploration of neural learning for discrete problems.
absZh: 我们提出了一种新的神经架构，用于学习输出序列的条件概率，其中输出元素是离散的标记，对应于输入序列中的位置。这类问题无法通过现有的序列到序列模型或神经图灵机直接解决，因为每一步输出的目标类别数量取决于输入的长度，而输入长度是可变的。排序可变长度序列以及各种组合优化问题都属于这一类。我们的模型利用最近提出的神经注意力机制解决了可变大小输出字典的问题。与之前的注意力机制不同，它不是在解码每一步将编码器的隐藏单元混合成一个上下文向量，而是将注意力用作指针，选择输入序列中的一个成员作为输出。我们将这种架构称为指针网络（Ptr-Net）。我们展示了Ptr-Nets可以仅通过训练示例学习解决三个具有挑战性的几何问题的近似解——寻找平面凸包、计算德劳内三角剖分以及平面旅行商问题。Ptr-Nets不仅改进了带有输入注意力的序列到序列模型，还允许我们推广到可变大小的输出字典。我们展示了学习模型能够泛化到超过其训练最大长度的序列。我们希望在这些任务上的结果能够鼓励对离散问题的神经学习的更广泛探索。

contrib.en: Proposes Pointer Networks (Ptr-Net), a new neural architecture using attention as a pointer to select input elements as outputs.
contrib.zh: 提出指针网络（Ptr-Net），一种使用注意力机制作为指针来选择输入元素作为输出的新型神经架构。

contrib.en: Addresses variable-size output dictionaries by using softmax distribution as a pointer, enabling handling of combinatorial problems.
contrib.zh: 通过使用softmax分布作为指针来解决可变大小输出字典的问题，从而能够处理组合优化问题。

contrib.en: Demonstrates Ptr-Net learns approximate solutions to planar convex hull, Delaunay triangulation, and TSP from training examples alone.
contrib.zh: 展示了Ptr-Net仅从训练示例中学习平面凸包、Delaunay三角剖分和旅行商问题的近似解。

contrib.en: Shows Ptr-Net generalizes to input lengths beyond those seen during training, outperforming sequence-to-sequence with attention.
contrib.zh: 展示了Ptr-Net能够泛化到训练时未见过的输入长度，优于带注意力的序列到序列模型。

contrib.en: Provides a purely data-driven approach to computationally intractable discrete problems, encouraging broader exploration.
contrib.zh: 提供了一种纯数据驱动的方法来处理计算上难以处理的离散问题，鼓励更广泛的探索。

limits.en: Ptr-Net's O(n^2) complexity limits scalability to very large inputs compared to exact algorithms.
limits.zh: Ptr-Net的O(n^2)复杂度限制了其相对于精确算法在大规模输入上的可扩展性。

limits.en: Model performance degrades for problems requiring complex reasoning, such as TSP beyond n=30.
limits.zh: 模型在需要复杂推理的问题上性能下降，例如n>30的旅行商问题。

limits.en: Input order affects accuracy; points appearing late in the sequence may be missed.
limits.zh: 输入顺序影响准确性；序列中较晚出现的点可能被遗漏。

limits.en: Model struggles with degenerate cases like collinear points in convex hull.
limits.zh: 模型在处理退化情况（如凸包中的共线点）时存在困难。

limits.en: Requires careful output ordering (e.g., triangle sorting) for effective learning; suboptimal ordering reduces performance.
limits.zh: 需要仔细的输出排序（例如三角形排序）才能有效学习；次优排序会降低性能。

items.en: We introduce a new neural architecture to learn the conditional probability of an output sequence with elements that are discrete tokens corresponding to positions in an input sequence. Such problems cannot be trivially addressed by existent approaches such as sequence-to-sequence [1] and Neural Turing Machines [2], because the number of target classes in each step of the output depends on the length of the input, which is variable. Problems such as sorting variable sized sequences, and various combinatorial optimization problems belong to this class. Our model solves the problem of variable size output dictionaries using a recently proposed mechanism of neural attention. It differs from the previous attention attempts in that, instead of using attention to blend hidden units of an encoder to a context vector at each decoder step, it uses attention as a pointer to select a member of the input sequence as the output. We call this architecture a Pointer Net (Ptr-Net). We show Ptr-Nets can be used to learn approximate solutions to three challenging geometric problems – finding planar convex hulls, computing Delaunay triangulations, and the planar Travelling Salesman Problem – using training examples alone. Ptr-Nets not only improve over sequence-to-sequence with input attention, but also allow us to generalize to variable size output dictionaries. We show that the learnt models generalize beyond the maximum lengths they were trained on. We hope our results on these tasks will encourage a broader exploration of neural learning for discrete problems.
items.zh: 我们提出了一种新的神经架构，用于学习输出序列的条件概率，其中输出序列的元素是对应于输入序列中位置的离散标记。此类问题无法通过现有方法（如序列到序列[1]和神经图灵机[2]）简单解决，因为输出每一步的目标类别数量取决于输入的长度，而输入长度是可变的。对可变大小序列进行排序以及各种组合优化问题都属于此类。我们的模型使用最近提出的神经注意力机制解决了可变大小输出字典的问题。它与之前的注意力尝试不同之处在于，不是使用注意力将编码器的隐藏单元混合成每个解码器步骤的上下文向量，而是将注意力用作指针来选择输入序列中的一个成员作为输出。我们将这种架构称为指针网络（Ptr-Net）。我们展示了Ptr-Nets可以仅使用训练示例来学习三个具有挑战性的几何问题的近似解——寻找平面凸包、计算德劳内三角剖分以及平面旅行商问题。Ptr-Nets不仅改进了带有输入注意力的序列到序列模型，还允许我们推广到可变大小的输出字典。我们展示了学习到的模型能够泛化到训练时未见过的更大长度。我们希望在这些任务上的结果将鼓励对离散问题的神经学习进行更广泛的探索。

items.en: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) have been used for learning functions over sequences from examples for more than three decades [3]. However, their architecture limited them to settings where the inputs and outputs were available at a fixed frame rate (e.g. [4]). The recently introduced sequence-to-sequence paradigm [1] removed these constraints by using one RNN to map an input sequence to an embedding and another (possibly the same) RNN to map the embedding to an output sequence. Bahdanau et. al. augmented the decoder by propagating extra contextual information from the input using a content-based attentional mechanism [5, 2, 6]. These developments have made it possible to apply RNNs to new domains, achieving state-of-the-art results in core problems in natural language processing such as translation [1, 5] and parsing [7], image and video captioning [8, 9], and even learning to execute small programs [2, 10].
items.zh: 循环神经网络（RNN）已被用于从示例中学习序列上的函数超过三十年[3]。然而，它们的架构将其限制在输入和输出以固定帧率可用的设置中（例如[4]）。最近引入的序列到序列范式[1]通过使用一个RNN将输入序列映射到嵌入，并使用另一个（可能相同）RNN将嵌入映射到输出序列，消除了这些限制。Bahdanau等人通过使用基于内容的注意力机制[5, 2, 6]从输入传播额外的上下文信息来增强解码器。这些发展使得将RNN应用于新领域成为可能，在自然语言处理的核心问题（如翻译[1, 5]和解析[7]）、图像和视频字幕生成[8, 9]，甚至学习执行小程序[2, 10]中取得了最先进的结果。

items.en: Nonetheless, these methods still require the size of the output dictionary to be fixed a priori. Because of this constraint we cannot directly apply this framework to combinatorial problems where the size of the output dictionary depends on the length of the input sequence. In this paper, we address this limitation by repurposing the attention mechanism of [5] to create pointers to input elements. We show that the resulting architecture, which we name Pointer Networks (Ptr-Nets), can be trained to output satisfactory solutions to three combinatorial optimization problems – computing planar convex hulls, Delaunay triangulations and the symmetric planar Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP). The resulting models produce approximate solutions to these problems in a purely data driven fashion (i.e., when we only have examples of inputs and desired outputs). The proposed approach is depicted in Figure 1.
items.zh: 尽管如此，这些方法仍然要求输出字典的大小事先固定。由于这一限制，我们无法直接将此框架应用于输出字典大小取决于输入序列长度的组合问题。在本文中，我们通过重新利用[5]中的注意力机制来创建指向输入元素的指针，从而解决了这一限制。我们展示了由此产生的架构，我们称之为指针网络（Ptr-Nets），可以训练输出三个组合优化问题的满意解——计算平面凸包、德劳内三角剖分和对称平面旅行商问题（TSP）。生成的模型以纯数据驱动的方式（即当我们只有输入和期望输出的示例时）产生这些问题的近似解。所提出的方法如图1所示。

items.en: The main contributions of our work are as follows:
items.zh: 我们工作的主要贡献如下：

items.en: We propose a new architecture, that we call Pointer Net, which is simple and effective. It deals with the fundamental problem of representing variable length dictionaries by using a softmax probability distribution as a “pointer”.
items.zh: 我们提出了一种新的架构，称为指针网络，它简单而有效。它通过使用softmax概率分布作为“指针”来处理表示可变长度字典的基本问题。

items.en: We apply the Pointer Net model to three distinct non-trivial algorithmic problems involving geometry. We show that the learned model generalizes to test problems with more points than the training problems.
items.zh: 我们将指针网络模型应用于三个涉及几何的不同非平凡算法问题。我们展示了学习到的模型能够泛化到比训练问题具有更多点的测试问题。

items.en: Our Pointer Net model learns a competitive small scale (n≤50𝑛50n\leq 50) TSP approximate solver. Our results demonstrate that a purely data driven approach can learn approximate solutions to problems that are computationally intractable.
items.zh: 我们的指针网络模型学习了一个有竞争力的小规模（n≤50）TSP近似求解器。我们的结果表明，纯数据驱动的方法可以学习计算上难以处理的问题的近似解。

items.en: We review the sequence-to-sequence [1] and input-attention models [5] that are the baselines for this work in Sections 2.1 and 2.2. We then describe our model - Ptr-Net in Section 2.3.
items.zh: 我们在第2.1节和第2.2节回顾了作为本工作基线的序列到序列[1]和输入注意力模型[5]。然后我们在第2.3节描述我们的模型——Ptr-Net。

items.en: Given a training pair, (𝒫,𝒞𝒫)𝒫superscript𝒞𝒫(\mathcal{P},\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}), the sequence-to-sequence model computes the conditional probability p​(𝒞𝒫|𝒫;θ)𝑝conditionalsuperscript𝒞𝒫𝒫𝜃p(\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}|\mathcal{P};\theta) using a parametric model (an RNN with parameters θ𝜃\theta) to estimate the terms of the probability chain rule (also see Figure 1), i.e.
items.zh: 给定一个训练对 (𝒫, 𝒞^𝒫)，序列到序列模型使用参数模型（一个参数为θ的RNN）计算条件概率 p(𝒞^𝒫|𝒫; θ)，以估计概率链式法则的项（也见图1），即

items.en: Here 𝒫={P1,…,Pn}𝒫subscript𝑃1…subscript𝑃𝑛\mathcal{P}=\{P_{1},\dots,P_{n}\} is a sequence of n𝑛n vectors and 𝒞𝒫={C1,…,Cm​(𝒫)}superscript𝒞𝒫subscript𝐶1…subscript𝐶𝑚𝒫\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}=\{C_{1},\dots,C_{m(\mathcal{P})}\} is a sequence of m​(𝒫)𝑚𝒫m(\mathcal{P}) indices, each between 1 and n𝑛n.
items.zh: 这里 𝒫 = {P₁, …, Pₙ} 是 n 个向量的序列，𝒞^𝒫 = {C₁, …, C_{m(𝒫)}} 是 m(𝒫) 个索引的序列，每个索引在 1 到 n 之间。

items.en: The parameters of the model are learnt by maximizing the conditional probabilities for the training set, i.e.
items.zh: 模型的参数通过最大化训练集的条件概率来学习，即

items.en: As in [1], we use an Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) [11] to model pθ​(Ci|C1,…,Ci−1,𝒫;θ)subscript𝑝𝜃conditionalsubscript𝐶𝑖subscript𝐶1…subscript𝐶𝑖1𝒫𝜃p_{\theta}(C_{i}|C_{1},\ldots,C_{i-1},\mathcal{P};\theta). The RNN is fed Pisubscript𝑃𝑖P_{i} at each time step, i𝑖i, until the end of the input sequence is reached, at which time a special symbol, ⇒⇒\Rightarrow is input to the model. The model then switches to the generation mode until the network encounters the special symbol ⇐⇐\Leftarrow, which represents termination of the output sequence.
items.zh: 如[1]中，我们使用长短期记忆网络（LSTM）[11]来建模 p_θ(C_i|C₁, …, C_{i-1}, 𝒫; θ)。RNN在每个时间步 i 被输入 P_i，直到输入序列结束，此时一个特殊符号 ⇒ 被输入模型。然后模型切换到生成模式，直到网络遇到特殊符号 ⇐，表示输出序列终止。

items.en: Note that this model makes no statistical independence assumptions. We use two separate RNNs (one to encode the sequence of vectors Pjsubscript𝑃𝑗P_{j}, and another one to produce or decode the output symbols Cisubscript𝐶𝑖C_{i}). We call the former RNN the encoder and the latter the decoder or the generative RNN.
items.zh: 注意，该模型没有做出统计独立性假设。我们使用两个独立的RNN（一个用于编码向量序列 P_j，另一个用于产生或解码输出符号 C_i）。我们将前一个RNN称为编码器，后一个称为解码器或生成RNN。

items.en: During inference, given a sequence 𝒫𝒫{\mathcal{P}}, the learnt parameters θ∗superscript𝜃\theta^{*} are used to select the sequence 𝒞^𝒫superscript^𝒞𝒫\mathcal{\hat{C}}^{\mathcal{P}} with the highest probability, i.e., 𝒞^𝒫=arg​max𝒞𝒫⁡p​(𝒞𝒫|𝒫;θ∗).superscript^𝒞𝒫subscriptargmaxsuperscript𝒞𝒫𝑝conditionalsuperscript𝒞𝒫𝒫superscript𝜃\displaystyle{\mathcal{\hat{C}}}^{\mathcal{P}}=\operatorname*{arg\,max}_{\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}}p(\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}|\mathcal{P};\theta^{*}). Finding the optimal sequence 𝒞^^𝒞\mathcal{\hat{C}} is computationally impractical because of the combinatorial number of possible output sequences. Instead we use a beam search procedure to find the best possible sequence given a beam size.
items.zh: 在推理过程中，给定一个序列 𝒫，学习到的参数 θ* 用于选择概率最高的序列 𝒞̂^𝒫，即 𝒞̂^𝒫 = arg max_{𝒞^𝒫} p(𝒞^𝒫|𝒫; θ*)。由于可能的输出序列数量组合爆炸，找到最优序列 𝒞̂ 在计算上不可行。相反，我们使用束搜索过程在给定束大小的情况下找到最佳可能序列。

items.en: In this sequence-to-sequence model, the output dictionary size for all symbols Cisubscript𝐶𝑖C_{i} is fixed and equal to n𝑛n, since the outputs are chosen from the input. Thus, we need to train a separate model for each n𝑛n. This prevents us from learning solutions to problems that have an output dictionary with a size that depends on the input sequence length.
items.zh: 在这个序列到序列模型中，所有符号 C_i 的输出字典大小固定且等于 n，因为输出是从输入中选择的。因此，我们需要为每个 n 训练一个单独的模型。这阻止了我们学习输出字典大小取决于输入序列长度的问题的解。

items.en: Under the assumption that the number of outputs is O​(n)𝑂𝑛O(n) this model has computational complexity of O​(n)𝑂𝑛O(n). However, exact algorithms for the problems we are dealing with are more costly. For example, the convex hull problem has complexity O​(n​log⁡n)𝑂𝑛𝑛O(n\log n). The attention mechanism (see Section 2.2) adds more “computational capacity” to this model.
items.zh: 假设输出数量为 O(n)，该模型的计算复杂度为 O(n)。然而，我们处理的问题的精确算法代价更高。例如，凸包问题的复杂度为 O(n log n)。注意力机制（见第2.2节）为此模型增加了更多的“计算能力”。

full.secZh: 2.2 基于内容的输入注意力

items.en: The vanilla sequence-to-sequence model produces the entire output sequence 𝒞𝒫superscript𝒞𝒫\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}} using the fixed dimensional state of the recognition RNN at the end of the input sequence 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}. This constrains the amount of information and computation that can flow through to the generative model. The attention model of [5] ameliorates this problem by augmenting the encoder and decoder RNNs with an additional neural network that uses an attention mechanism over the entire sequence of encoder RNN states.
items.zh: 原始的序列到序列模型使用输入序列 𝒫 结束时识别RNN的固定维度状态来生成整个输出序列 𝒞^𝒫。这限制了可以流向生成模型的信息量和计算量。[5]的注意力模型通过使用一个额外的神经网络来缓解这个问题，该网络在整个编码器RNN状态序列上使用注意力机制。

items.en: For notation purposes, let us define the encoder and decoder hidden states as (e1,…,en)subscript𝑒1…subscript𝑒𝑛(e_{1},\ldots,e_{n}) and (d1,…,dm​(𝒫))subscript𝑑1…subscript𝑑𝑚𝒫(d_{1},\ldots,d_{m(\mathcal{P})}), respectively. For the LSTM RNNs, we use the state after the output gate has been component-wise multiplied by the cell activations. We compute the attention vector at each output time i𝑖i as follows:
items.zh: 为了符号表示，让我们将编码器和解码器隐藏状态分别定义为 (e₁, …, eₙ) 和 (d₁, …, d_{m(𝒫)})。对于LSTM RNN，我们使用输出门与细胞激活逐元素相乘后的状态。我们在每个输出时间 i 计算注意力向量如下：

items.en: where softmax normalizes the vector uisuperscript𝑢𝑖u^{i} (of length n𝑛n) to be the “attention” mask over the inputs, and v𝑣v, W1subscript𝑊1W_{1}, and W2subscript𝑊2W_{2} are learnable parameters of the model. In all our experiments, we use the same hidden dimensionality at the encoder and decoder (typically 512), so v𝑣v is a vector and W1subscript𝑊1W_{1} and W2subscript𝑊2W_{2} are square matrices. Lastly, di′subscriptsuperscript𝑑′𝑖d^{\prime}_{i} and disubscript𝑑𝑖d_{i} are concatenated and used as the hidden states from which we make predictions and which we feed to the next time step in the recurrent model.
items.zh: 其中 softmax 将向量 u^i（长度为 n）归一化为输入上的“注意力”掩码，而 v、W1 和 W2 是模型的可学习参数。在所有实验中，我们在编码器和解码器中使用相同的隐藏维度（通常为 512），因此 v 是一个向量，W1 和 W2 是方阵。最后，d'_i 和 d_i 被拼接起来，用作进行预测的隐藏状态，并作为循环模型中下一时间步的输入。

items.en: Note that for each output we have to perform n𝑛n operations, so the computational complexity at inference time becomes O​(n2)𝑂superscript𝑛2O(n^{2}).
items.zh: 注意，对于每个输出，我们必须执行 n 次操作，因此推理时的计算复杂度变为 O(n^2)。

items.en: This model performs significantly better than the sequence-to-sequence model on the convex hull problem, but it is not applicable to problems where the output dictionary size depends on the input.
items.zh: 该模型在凸包问题上的表现显著优于序列到序列模型，但它不适用于输出字典大小取决于输入的问题。

items.en: Nevertheless, a very simple extension (or rather reduction) of the model allows us to do this easily.
items.zh: 然而，一个非常简单的扩展（或者说简化）模型使我们能够轻松做到这一点。

items.en: We now describe a very simple modification of the attention model that allows us to apply the method to solve combinatorial optimization problems where the output dictionary size depends on the number of elements in the input sequence.
items.zh: 我们现在描述注意力模型的一个非常简单的修改，使我们能够将该方法应用于解决输出字典大小取决于输入序列元素数量的组合优化问题。

items.en: The sequence-to-sequence model of Section 2.1 uses a softmax distribution over a fixed sized output dictionary to compute p​(Ci|C1,…,Ci−1,𝒫)𝑝conditionalsubscript𝐶𝑖subscript𝐶1…subscript𝐶𝑖1𝒫p(C_{i}|C_{1},\ldots,C_{i-1},\mathcal{P}) in Equation 1. Thus it cannot be used for our problems where the size of the output dictionary is equal to the length of the input sequence. To solve this problem we model p​(Ci|C1,…,Ci−1,𝒫)𝑝conditionalsubscript𝐶𝑖subscript𝐶1…subscript𝐶𝑖1𝒫p(C_{i}|C_{1},\ldots,C_{i-1},\mathcal{P}) using the attention mechanism of Equation 3 as follows:
items.zh: 其中 softmax 将向量 u^i（长度为 n）归一化为输入字典上的输出分布，而 v、W1 和 W2 是输出模型的可学习参数。这里，我们不混合编码器状态 e_j 以向解码器传播额外信息，而是使用 u^i_j 作为指向输入元素的指针。类似地，为了像公式 1 那样以 C_{i-1} 为条件，我们只需将相应的 P_{C_{i-1}} 复制作为输入。我们的方法和注意力模型都可以看作是 [6,5,2] 中提出的基于内容的注意力机制的应用。

items.en: where softmax normalizes the vector uisuperscript𝑢𝑖u^{i} (of length n𝑛n) to be an output distribution over the dictionary of inputs, and v𝑣v, W1subscript𝑊1W_{1}, and W2subscript𝑊2W_{2} are learnable parameters of the output model. Here, we do not blend the encoder state ejsubscript𝑒𝑗e_{j} to propagate extra information to the decoder, but instead, use ujisubscriptsuperscript𝑢𝑖𝑗u^{i}_{j} as pointers to the input elements. In a similar way, to condition on Ci−1subscript𝐶𝑖1C_{i-1} as in Equation 1, we simply copy the corresponding PCi−1subscript𝑃subscript𝐶𝑖1P_{C_{i-1}} as the input. Both our method and the attention model can be seen as an application of content-based attention mechanisms proposed in [6, 5, 2].
items.zh: 我们还注意到，我们的方法专门针对输出是离散的且对应于输入中位置的问题。这些问题可以人为地解决——例如，我们可以学习使用 RNN 直接输出目标点的坐标。然而，在推理时，这种解决方案不满足输出精确映射回输入的约束。没有约束，预测在较长序列上会变得模糊，如视频的序列到序列模型所示 [12]。

items.en: We also note that our approach specifically targets problems whose outputs are discrete and correspond to positions in the input. Such problems may be addressed artificially – for example we could learn to output the coordinates of the target point directly using an RNN. However, at inference, this solution does not respect the constraint that the outputs map back to the inputs exactly. Without the constraints, the predictions are bound to become blurry over longer sequences as shown in sequence-to-sequence models for videos [12].
items.zh: 在以下部分中，我们回顾了我们考虑的每个问题以及我们的数据生成协议。

items.en: In the following sections, we review each of the three problems we considered, as well as our data generation protocol.111We release all the datasets at http://goo.gl/NDcOIG.
items.zh: 在训练数据中，输入是平面点集 P = {P_1, ..., P_n}，每个有 n 个元素，其中 P_j = (x_j, y_j) 是点的笛卡尔坐标，我们在此基础上寻找凸包、Delaunay 三角剖分或相应的旅行商问题的解。在所有情况下，我们从 [0,1] × [0,1] 上的均匀分布中采样。输出 C^P = {C_1, ..., C_{m(P)}} 是表示与点集 P 相关的解的序列。在图 2 中，我们找到了凸包和 Delaunay 问题的输入/输出对 (P, C^P) 的图示。

items.en: In the training data, the inputs are planar point sets 𝒫={P1,…,Pn}𝒫subscript𝑃1…subscript𝑃𝑛\mathcal{P}=\{P_{1},\dots,P_{n}\} with n𝑛n elements each, where Pj=(xj,yj)subscript𝑃𝑗subscript𝑥𝑗subscript𝑦𝑗P_{j}=(x_{j},y_{j}) are the cartesian coordinates of the points over which we find the convex hull, the Delaunay triangulation or the solution to the corresponding Travelling Salesman Problem. In all cases, we sample from a uniform distribution in [0,1]×[0,1]0101[0,1]\times[0,1]. The outputs 𝒞𝒫={C1,…,Cm​(𝒫)}superscript𝒞𝒫subscript𝐶1…subscript𝐶𝑚𝒫\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}=\{C_{1},\dots,C_{m(\mathcal{P})}\} are sequences representing the solution associated to the point set 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}. In Figure 2, we find an illustration of an input/output pair (𝒫,𝒞𝒫)𝒫superscript𝒞𝒫(\mathcal{P},\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}) for the convex hull and the Delaunay problems.
items.zh: 图 2： (a) 凸包和 (b) Delaunay 三角剖分的输入/输出表示。标记 ⇒ 和 ⇐ 分别表示序列的开始和结束。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Input/output representation for (a) convex hull and (b) Delaunay triangulation. The tokens ⇒⇒\Rightarrow and ⇐⇐\Leftarrow represent beginning and end of sequence, respectively.
items.capZh: 我们使用这个例子作为基线来开发我们的模型，并理解用数据驱动方法解决组合问题的难度。寻找有限点集的凸包是计算几何中一个非常理解的任务，并且有几种精确解可用（参见 [13,14,15]）。通常，寻找（通常唯一的）解的复杂度为 O(n log n)，其中 n 是考虑的点数。

items.en: We used this example as a baseline to develop our models and to understand the difficulty of solving combinatorial problems with data driven approaches. Finding the convex hull of a finite number of points is a well understood task in computational geometry, and there are several exact solutions available (see [13, 14, 15]). In general, finding the (generally unique) solution has complexity O​(n​log⁡n)𝑂𝑛𝑛O(n\log n), where n𝑛n is the number of points considered.
items.zh: 向量 P_j 从 [0,1] × [0,1] 中均匀采样。元素 C_i 是 1 到 n 之间的索引，对应于序列 P 中的位置，或者是表示序列开始或结束的特殊标记。参见图 2 (a) 的图示。为了将输出表示为序列，我们从索引最低的点开始，逆时针方向——这是一个任意的选择，但有助于减少训练期间的歧义。

items.en: The vectors 𝒫jsubscript𝒫𝑗\mathcal{P}_{j} are uniformly sampled from [0,1]×[0,1]0101[0,1]\times[0,1]. The elements Cisubscript𝐶𝑖C_{i} are indices between 1 and n𝑛n corresponding to positions in the sequence 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}, or special tokens representing beginning or end of sequence. See Figure 2 (a) for an illustration. To represent the output as a sequence, we start from the point with the lowest index, and go counter-clockwise – this is an arbitrary choice but helps reducing ambiguities during training.
items.zh: 对于平面中的点集 P，Delaunay 三角剖分是一种三角剖分，使得每个三角形的外接圆都是空的，即其内部没有 P 中的点。存在精确的 O(n log n) 解 [16]，其中 n 是 P 中的点数。

items.en: A Delaunay triangulation for a set 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P} of points in a plane is a triangulation such that each circumcircle of every triangle is empty, that is, there is no point from 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P} in its interior. Exact O​(n​log⁡n)𝑂𝑛𝑛O(n\log n) solutions are available [16], where n𝑛n is the number of points in 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}.
items.zh: 在这个例子中，输出 C^P = {C_1, ..., C_{m(P)}} 是表示点集 P 的三角剖分的相应序列。每个 C_i 是一个从 1 到 n 的三元组整数，对应于三角形顶点在 P 中的位置，或者是开始/结束序列标记。参见图 2 (b)。

items.en: In this example, the outputs 𝒞𝒫={C1,…,Cm​(𝒫)}superscript𝒞𝒫subscript𝐶1…subscript𝐶𝑚𝒫\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}=\{C_{1},\dots,C_{m(\mathcal{P})}\} are the corresponding sequences representing the triangulation of the point set 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P} . Each Cisubscript𝐶𝑖C_{i} is a triple of integers from 1 to n𝑛n corresponding to the position of triangle vertices in 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P} or the beginning/end of sequence tokens. See Figure 2 (b).
items.zh: 我们注意到，序列 C^P 的任何排列都表示 P 的相同三角剖分，此外，每个由三个整数组成的三角形表示 C_i 也可以排列。不失一般性，类似于我们在训练时对凸包所做的，我们按三角形内心坐标（字典序）对三角形 C_i 进行排序，并选择递增的三角形表示。如果没有排序，学习到的模型效果不佳，找到比 Ptr-Net 能更好利用的排序是未来工作的一部分。

items.en: We note that any permutation of the sequence 𝒞𝒫superscript𝒞𝒫\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}} represents the same triangulation for 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}, additionally each triangle representation Cisubscript𝐶𝑖C_{i} of three integers can also be permuted. Without loss of generality, and similarly to what we did for convex hulls at training time, we order the triangles Cisubscript𝐶𝑖C_{i} by their incenter coordinates (lexicographic order) and choose the increasing triangle representation222We choose Ci=(1,2,4)subscript𝐶𝑖124C_{i}=(1,2,4) instead of (2,4,1) or any other permutation.. Without ordering, the models learned were not as good, and finding a better ordering that the Ptr-Net could better exploit is part of future work.
items.zh: TSP 出现在理论计算机科学的许多领域，是用于微芯片设计或 DNA 测序的重要算法。在我们的工作中，我们专注于平面对称 TSP：给定一个城市列表，我们希望找到访问每个城市恰好一次并返回起点的最短可能路线。此外，我们假设两个城市之间的距离在每个相反方向上是相同的。这是一个 NP 难问题，使我们能够测试我们模型的能力和局限性。

items.en: TSP arises in many areas of theoretical computer science and is an important algorithm used for microchip design or DNA sequencing. In our work we focused on the planar symmetric TSP: given a list of cities, we wish to find the shortest possible route that visits each city exactly once and returns to the starting point. Additionally, we assume the distance between two cities is the same in each opposite direction. This is an NP-hard problem which allows us to test the capabilities and limitations of our model.
items.zh: 输入/输出对 (P, C^P) 的格式与第 3.1 节描述的凸包问题类似。P 将是表示城市的笛卡尔坐标，这些坐标在 [0,1] × [0,1] 正方形中随机选择。C^P = {C_1, ..., C_n} 将是 1 到 n 的整数排列，表示最优路径（或旅行）。为了一致性，在训练数据集中，我们总是从第一个城市开始，不失一般性。

items.en: The input/output pairs (𝒫,𝒞𝒫)𝒫superscript𝒞𝒫(\mathcal{P},\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}) have a similar format as in the Convex Hull problem described in Section 3.1. 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P} will be the cartesian coordinates representing the cities, which are chosen randomly in the [0,1]×[0,1]0101[0,1]\times[0,1] square. 𝒞𝒫={C1,…,Cn}superscript𝒞𝒫subscript𝐶1…subscript𝐶𝑛\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}}=\{C_{1},\dots,C_{n}\} will be a permutation of integers from 1 to n𝑛n representing the optimal path (or tour). For consistency, in the training dataset, we always start in the first city without loss of generality.
items.zh: 为了生成精确数据，我们实现了 Held-Karp 算法 [17]，该算法在 O(2^n n^2) 内找到最优解（我们将其用于 n 最大为 20）。对于更大的 n，生成精确解极其昂贵，因此我们还考虑了产生近似解的算法：A1 [18] 和 A2 [19]，两者都是 O(n^2)，以及 A3 [20]，它实现了 O(n^3) 的 Christofides 算法。后一种算法保证找到的解在最优长度的 1.5 倍以内。表 2 显示了它们在我们的测试集中的表现。

items.en: To generate exact data, we implemented the Held-Karp algorithm [17] which finds the optimal solution in O​(2n​n2)𝑂superscript2𝑛superscript𝑛2O(2^{n}n^{2}) (we used it up to n=20𝑛20n=20). For larger n𝑛n, producing exact solutions is extremely costly, therefore we also considered algorithms that produce approximated solutions: A1 [18] and A2 [19], which are both O​(n2)𝑂superscript𝑛2O(n^{2}), and A3 [20] which implements the O​(n3)𝑂superscript𝑛3O(n^{3}) Christofides algorithm. The latter algorithm is guaranteed to find a solution within a factor of 1.5 from the optimal length. Table 2 shows how they performed in our test sets.
items.zh: 在本文介绍的工作中，没有对 Ptr-Net 进行广泛的架构或超参数搜索，我们在所有实验和数据集上使用了几乎相同的架构。尽管通过调整模型可能会获得一些收益，但我们认为在所有问题上使用相同的模型超参数会使论文的主要信息更加强烈。

items.en: No extensive architecture or hyperparameter search of the Ptr-Net was done in the work presented here, and we used virtually the same architecture throughout all the experiments and datasets. Even though there are likely some gains to be obtained by tuning the model, we felt that having the same model hyperparameters operate on all the problems would make the main message of the paper stronger.

As a result, all our models used a single layer LSTM with either 256 or 512 hidden units, trained with stochastic gradient descent with a learning rate of 1.0, batch size of 128, random uniform weight initialization from -0.08 to 0.08, and L2 gradient clipping of 2.0. We generated 1M training example pairs, and we did observe overfitting in some cases where the task was simpler (i.e., for small n𝑛n).
items.zh: 因此，我们所有模型均使用单层LSTM，隐藏单元数为256或512，采用随机梯度下降训练，学习率为1.0，批量大小为128，权重从-0.08到0.08均匀随机初始化，L2梯度裁剪为2.0。我们生成了100万对训练样本，并在任务较简单（即n较小）的某些情况下观察到了过拟合。

items.en: We used the convex hull as the guiding task which allowed us to understand the deficiencies of standard models such as the sequence-to-sequence approach, and also setting up our expectations on what a purely data driven model would be able to achieve with respect to an exact solution.
items.zh: 我们使用凸包作为引导任务，这使我们能够理解标准模型（如序列到序列方法）的缺陷，并建立对纯数据驱动模型在精确解方面所能达到效果的预期。

items.en: We reported two metrics: accuracy, and area covered of the true convex hull (note that any simple polygon will have full intersection with the true convex hull). To compute the accuracy, we considered two output sequences 𝒞1superscript𝒞1\mathcal{C}^{1} and 𝒞2superscript𝒞2\mathcal{C}^{2} to be the same if they represent the same polygon. For simplicity, we only computed the area coverage for the test examples in which the output represents a simple polygon (i.e., without self-intersections). If an algorithm fails to produce a simple polygon in more than 1% of the cases, we simply reported FAIL.
items.zh: 我们报告了两个指标：准确率和真实凸包的面积覆盖率（注意，任何简单多边形与真实凸包的交集都是完整的）。为计算准确率，如果两个输出序列𝒞¹和𝒞²表示相同的多边形，则认为它们相同。为简化起见，我们仅计算输出为简单多边形（即无自交）的测试样本的面积覆盖率。如果某个算法在超过1%的情况下未能生成简单多边形，我们直接报告FAIL。

items.en: The results are presented in Table 1. We note that the area coverage achieved with the Ptr-Net is close to 100%. Looking at examples of mistakes, we see that most problems come from points that are aligned (see Figure 3 (d) for a mistake for n=500𝑛500n=500) – this is a common source of errors in most algorithms to solve the convex hull.
items.zh: 结果如表1所示。我们注意到，使用Ptr-Net实现的面积覆盖率接近100%。观察错误案例，我们发现大多数问题源于共线的点（见图3(d)，n=500时的错误）——这是大多数凸包求解算法常见的错误来源。

items.en: It was seen that the order in which the inputs are presented to the encoder during inference affects its performance. When the points on the true convex hull are seen “late” in the input sequence, the accuracy is lower. This is possibly the network does not have enough processing steps to “update” the convex hull it computed until the latest points were seen. In order to overcome this problem, we used the attention mechanism described in Section 2.2, which allows the decoder to look at the whole input at any time. This modification boosted the model performance significantly. We inspected what attention was focusing on, and we observed that it was “pointing” at the correct answer on the input side. This inspired us to create the Ptr-Net model described in Section 2.3.
items.zh: 我们发现，推理时输入呈现给编码器的顺序会影响其性能。当真实凸包上的点在输入序列中“较晚”出现时，准确率较低。这可能是因为网络没有足够的处理步骤来“更新”其计算的凸包，直到看到最新的点。为解决此问题，我们使用了第2.2节描述的注意力机制，该机制允许解码器随时查看整个输入。这一改进显著提升了模型性能。我们检查了注意力聚焦的位置，发现它“指向”了输入侧的正确答案。这启发我们创建了第2.3节描述的Ptr-Net模型。

items.en: More than outperforming both the LSTM and the LSTM with attention, our model has the key advantage of being inherently variable length. The bottom half of Table 1 shows that, when training our model on a variety of lengths ranging from 5 to 50 (uniformly sampled, as we found other forms of curriculum learning to not be effective), a single model is able to perform quite well on all lengths it has been trained on (but some degradation for n=50𝑛50n=50 can be observed w.r.t. the model trained only on length 50 instances). More impressive is the fact that the model does extrapolate to lengths that it has never seen during training. Even for n=500𝑛500n=500, our results are satisfactory and indirectly indicate that the model has learned more than a simple lookup. Neither LSTM or LSTM with attention can be used for any given n′≠nsuperscript𝑛′𝑛n^{\prime}\neq n without training a new model on n′superscript𝑛′n^{\prime}.
items.zh: 除了优于LSTM和带注意力的LSTM外，我们的模型还有一个关键优势：天生支持可变长度。表1的下半部分显示，当我们在5到50的多种长度上训练模型时（均匀采样，因为我们发现其他形式的课程学习无效），单个模型能在其训练过的所有长度上表现良好（但相对于仅在长度为50的实例上训练的模型，n=50时略有退化）。更令人印象深刻的是，该模型能外推到训练中从未见过的长度。即使对于n=500，我们的结果也令人满意，间接表明模型学到的不仅仅是简单的查找。无论是LSTM还是带注意力的LSTM，都无法在不针对n'训练新模型的情况下用于任意n'≠n。

items.en: The Delaunay Triangulation test case is connected to our first problem of finding the convex hull. In fact, the Delaunay Triangulation for a given set of points triangulates the convex hull of these points.
items.zh: Delaunay三角剖分测试用例与我们第一个寻找凸包的问题相关。实际上，对于给定点集，Delaunay三角剖分是对这些点的凸包进行三角剖分。

items.en: We reported two metrics: accuracy and triangle coverage in percentage (the percentage of triangles the model predicted correctly). Note that, in this case, for an input point set 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}, the output sequence 𝒞​(𝒫)𝒞𝒫\mathcal{C}(\mathcal{P}) is, in fact, a set. As a consequence, any permutation of its elements will represent the same triangulation.
items.zh: 我们报告了两个指标：准确率和三角形覆盖率（百分比，即模型正确预测的三角形比例）。注意，在这种情况下，对于输入点集𝒫，输出序列𝒞(𝒫)实际上是一个集合。因此，其元素的任何排列都表示相同的三角剖分。

items.en: Using the Ptr-Net model for n=5𝑛5n=5, we obtained an accuracy of 80.7% and triangle coverage of 93.0%. For n=10𝑛10n=10, the accuracy was 22.6% and the triangle coverage 81.3%. For n=50𝑛50n=50, we did not produce any precisely correct triangulation, but obtained 52.8% triangle coverage. See the middle column of Figure 3 for an example for n=50𝑛50n=50.
items.zh: 使用Ptr-Net模型，对于n=5，我们获得了80.7%的准确率和93.0%的三角形覆盖率。对于n=10，准确率为22.6%，三角形覆盖率为81.3%。对于n=50，我们没有产生任何完全正确的三角剖分，但获得了52.8%的三角形覆盖率。参见图3中间列n=50的示例。

items.en: We considered the planar symmetric travelling salesman problem (TSP), which is NP-hard as the third problem. Similarly to finding convex hulls, it also has sequential outputs. Given that the Ptr-Net implements an O​(n2)𝑂superscript𝑛2O(n^{2}) algorithm, it was unclear if it would have enough capacity to learn a useful algorithm solely from data.
items.zh: 我们考虑了平面对称旅行商问题（TSP），这是第三个NP难问题。与寻找凸包类似，它也有序列输出。鉴于Ptr-Net实现的是O(n²)算法，尚不清楚它是否有足够的能力仅从数据中学习有用的算法。

items.en: As discussed in Section 3.3, it is feasible to generate exact solutions for relatively small values of n𝑛n to be used as training data. For larger n𝑛n, due to the importance of TSP, good and efficient algorithms providing reasonable approximate solutions exist. We used three different algorithms in our experiments – A1, A2, and A3 (see Section 3.3 for references).
items.zh: 如第3.3节所述，对于相对较小的n，生成精确解作为训练数据是可行的。对于较大的n，由于TSP的重要性，存在良好且高效的算法提供合理的近似解。我们在实验中使用了三种不同的算法——A1、A2和A3（参考文献见第3.3节）。

items.en: The first group of rows in the table show the Ptr-Net trained on optimal data, except for n=50𝑛50n=50, since that is not feasible computationally (we trained a separate model for each n𝑛n). Interestingly, when using the worst algorithm (A1) data to train the Ptr-Net, our model outperforms the algorithm that is trying to imitate.
items.zh: 表中第一组行显示了在最优数据上训练的Ptr-Net，但n=50除外，因为这在计算上不可行（我们为每个n训练了单独的模型）。有趣的是，当使用最差算法（A1）的数据训练Ptr-Net时，我们的模型优于它试图模仿的算法。

items.en: The second group of rows in the table show how the Ptr-Net trained on optimal data with 5 to 20 cities can generalize beyond that. The results are virtually perfect for n=25𝑛25n=25, and good for n=30𝑛30n=30, but it seems to break for 40 and beyond (still, the results are far better than chance). This contrasts with the convex hull case, where we were able to generalize by a factor of 10. However, the underlying algorithms are of far greater complexity than O​(n​log⁡n)𝑂𝑛𝑛O(n\log n), which could explain this phenomenon.
items.zh: 表中第二组行显示了在5到20个城市的最优数据上训练的Ptr-Net如何泛化到更大规模。对于n=25，结果几乎完美；对于n=30，结果良好；但对于40及以上，似乎失效（尽管如此，结果仍远优于随机）。这与凸包情况形成对比，后者我们能够泛化10倍。然而，底层算法的复杂度远高于O(n log n)，这可能解释了这一现象。

items.en: In this paper we described Ptr-Net, a new architecture that allows us to learn a conditional probability of one sequence 𝒞𝒫superscript𝒞𝒫\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}} given another sequence 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}, where 𝒞𝒫superscript𝒞𝒫\mathcal{C}^{\mathcal{P}} is a sequence of discrete tokens corresponding to positions in 𝒫𝒫\mathcal{P}. We show that Ptr-Nets can be used to learn solutions to three different combinatorial optimization problems. Our method works on variable sized inputs (yielding variable sized output dictionaries), something the baseline models (sequence-to-sequence with or without attention) cannot do directly. Even more impressively, they outperform the baselines on fixed input size problems - to which both the models can be applied.
items.zh: 本文描述了Ptr-Net，一种新架构，允许我们学习给定序列𝒫条件下另一序列𝒞^𝒫的条件概率，其中𝒞^𝒫是对应于𝒫中位置的离散标记序列。我们展示了Ptr-Net可用于学习三种不同组合优化问题的解。我们的方法适用于可变大小的输入（产生可变大小的输出字典），这是基线模型（带或不带注意力的序列到序列）无法直接做到的。更令人印象深刻的是，在固定输入大小的问题上，它们也优于基线模型——而这两种模型都可以应用于固定输入大小的问题。

items.en: Previous methods such as RNNSearch, Memory Networks and Neural Turing Machines [5, 6, 2] have used attention mechanisms to process inputs. However these methods do not directly address problems that arise with variable output dictionaries. We have shown that an attention mechanism can be applied to the output to solve such problems. In so doing, we have opened up a new class of problems to which neural networks can be applied without artificial assumptions. In this paper, we have applied this extension to RNNSearch, but the methods are equally applicable to Memory Networks and Neural Turing Machines.
items.zh: 先前的方法如RNNSearch、记忆网络和神经图灵机[5, 6, 2]已使用注意力机制处理输入。但这些方法并未直接解决可变输出字典带来的问题。我们证明了注意力机制可应用于输出来解决此类问题。由此，我们开辟了一类新问题，神经网络可在无需人为假设的情况下应用于这些问题。本文中我们将此扩展应用于RNNSearch，但该方法同样适用于记忆网络和神经图灵机。

items.en: Future work will try and show its applicability to other problems such as sorting where the outputs are chosen from the inputs. We are also excited about the possibility of using this approach to other combinatorial optimization problems.
items.zh: 未来工作将尝试展示其在其他问题（如从输入中选择输出的排序问题）中的适用性。我们也对将该方法用于其他组合优化问题的可能性感到兴奋。

items.en: We would like to thank Rafal Jozefowicz, Ilya Sutskever, Quoc Le and Samy Bengio for useful discussions on this topic. We would also like to thank Daniel Gillick for his help with the final manuscript.
items.zh: 感谢Rafal Jozefowicz、Ilya Sutskever、Quoc Le和Samy Bengio在本课题上的有益讨论。同时感谢Daniel Gillick对最终稿的帮助。
